title: Hiring Panel — VP Engineering (Jasper Moreau)
compiled_truth:
Panel convened at 2:00 PM on August 15, 2027 in the Anchor Codec downtown office, Conference Room B. Attendees were [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) (CEO), [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) (Head of Infrastructure), and [Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) (Principal Engineer). The candidate, Jasper Moreau, interviewed for the VP Engineering role — a position that has been open since June when the previous engineering lead departed during the Series B ramp.

Seren opened the debrief by noting that Moreau's background in distributed systems and his seven years leading platform teams at mid-stage startups made him a strong fit on paper. His references from two prior companies were uniformly positive, particularly around his ability to scale engineering orgs from 15 to 60+ while maintaining shipping velocity. Seren flagged one concern: Moreau had no direct experience with deterministic data infrastructure, and [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) is entering a phase where the VP Eng needs to deeply understand single-pass primitives to make sound architectural trade-offs.

Dani's assessment was more measured. He said Moreau asked sharp questions during the systems design portion — particularly around failure modes in compression pipelines and how authentication is validated inline. Dani noted Moreau's instinct was to default to eventual consistency patterns, which doesn't align well with Anchor's deterministic guarantees. "He's trainable, but the learning curve is real," Dani said. He also raised a mild concern about Moreau's management philosophy leaning heavily toward process-driven frameworks, which could clash with the current team's more ad-hoc engineering culture.

Tal was the most enthusiastic. He said the coding exercise went well — Moreau wrote clean, idiomatic code and communicated his reasoning clearly throughout. Tal pushed back on Dani's consistency concern, arguing that Moreau demonstrated intellectual curiosity and was quick to adapt when Tal introduced constraints specific to single-pass architectures. Tal also pointed out that nobody currently on the team has scaled an org past 30 engineers, and that's exactly what Anchor needs in the next 12 months.

The panel voted 2-1 to advance Moreau to a final round with the board advisor. Dani was the dissenting vote, recommending they also bring in at least one more candidate before making an offer. Seren agreed to a comprimise: advance Moreau to finals but keep the req open for two more weeks to source additional candidates. Final round is tentatively scheduled for August 28.
timeline: - **2027-06-02** | VP Engineering role posted after departure of previous engineering lead at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor)
- **2027-06-19** | Initial sourcing round yields 14 candidates; recruiter narrows to 5 for phone screens
- **2027-07-08** | Jasper Moreau passes phone screen with [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei), advances to on-site
- **2027-07-22** | Moreau completes take-home systems design exercise
- **2027-08-04** | On-site interview day with Moreau — technical deep-dive and team culture sessions
- **2027-08-15** | Hiring panel convenes; [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss), Dani, and [Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) debrief and vote 2-1 to advance
- **2027-08-16** | Seren emails board advisor to schedule final-round interview for Moreau
- **2027-08-28** | Final round interview with board advisor (tentative)
